rulings.Case Title: LOUIS REYES VS. GENUINE PARTS COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Default And To Allow The Filing Of Proposed Answer With Declaration And Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 30, 2015, line 1. Defendant Floyd's Auto Parts, Inc.'s motion for order vacating and setting aside default and to allow the filing of proposed answer pursuant to C.C.P. section 473(b) is granted. No opposition filed. The default entered December 9, 2014 is vacated. Defendant has 10 days to file its answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)

Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 30, 2015, line 2. Defendant Union Pacific Railroad Company's special demurrer to plaintiff's first and second causes of action is sustained. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose the demurrer. The demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiff the opportunity to cure the defects identified by the demurrer. Specifically, plaintiff has leave to amend to allege sufficient facts to meet the standards set forth in Campbell v. Ford Motor Co., (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 15 and Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/TLJ)
